title: Due Time
subtitle: Ready for birth
description: By week 40, you have reached your due date. Your baby is fully developed and ready to be born. Labor may begin naturally at any time, or your doctor may guide next steps if needed.
what_to_expect: Fetal movements continue but may feel restricted due to limited space
Labor can begin at any time if not already started
You may experience strong and regular contractions or other labor signs
care_tips: Stay calm and follow your doctor’s guidance
Maintain light meals and stay hydrated
Keep your hospital bag and essentials ready

medical_checkups.note: Close monitoring is essential if delivery has not yet occurred.

commonly_suggested.name: Fetal Monitoring (Non-Stress Test if advised)
commonly_suggested.purpose: Checks baby’s heart rate and activity

medical_checkups.guidance: Your doctor may discuss induction or further monitoring if labor does not begin naturally.

important_note: Every pregnancy is unique, and delivery timing can vary slightly beyond the due date.
reassurance: Your baby is ready, and you are at the final step of your journey.
medical_disclaimer: Contact your healthcare provider immediately if you notice contractions, water breaking, or any unusual symptoms.

diabetes.personalized_description: Maintaining stable blood sugar levels is critical during labor and delivery.
care_tips: Follow your doctor’s dietary and medical guidance
diabetes.medical_focus: Blood sugar levels will be closely monitored during labor.
diabetes.reassurance: Careful management supports a safe delivery.

anemia.personalized_description: Adequate iron levels support your body during delivery and recovery.
anemia.medical_focus: Hemoglobin levels may be reviewed before or during delivery.
anemia.reassurance: Supporting your nutrition helps prepare your body for childbirth.

hypertension.personalized_description: Stable blood pressure is essential for a safe labor and delivery process.
hypertension.medical_focus: Blood pressure will be closely monitored during labor.
hypertension.reassurance: A calm and steady approach supports a safer delivery.

thyroid.personalized_description: Balanced thyroid levels support stability during labor and immediate recovery.
thyroid.medical_focus: Thyroid function may be reviewed as needed during delivery.
thyroid.reassurance: Consistency helps maintain balance during this important time.

multi_condition_blend.combined_message: At this final stage, managing multiple conditions requires close coordination with your healthcare provider.
priority_tips: Avoid any sudden lifestyle or dietary changes
multi_condition_blend.reassurance: You are ready, and steady care will support a safe and successful delivery.
